Description: The axis mapping the relationship between external physical reality and internal mental representation, distinguishing verifiable material states from qualitative experience.
Related Cognitive/Psychological Concept: Intersubjectivity; Phenomenology; Cognitive vs. Emotional Intelligence (IQ vs. EI).
Classic FEP Capability: Bayesian inference and active inference regarding external sensory causes to minimize surprisal.
FEP+ Extension Detail: Incorporates emotional processing, qualia modeling, and subjective agency (Selfons) into the generative model; maps how private semantic wavefunctions phase-shift into public gauge fields.

Description: The axis defining information sharing and accessibility, moving from individual internal states to shared social, cultural, and systemic environments.
Related Cognitive/Psychological Concept: Intersubjectivity; Collective intelligence; Theory of Mind; Cultural normativity.
Classic FEP Capability: Minimization of individual surprisal within a single Markov blanket.
FEP+ Extension Detail: Extends to collective meta-states and intersubjective synergy; utilizes sheaf-theoretic gluing and "Memetrons" to model cultural transmission and shared symbolic protocols beyond the single-agent loop.

Description: The axis tracking the stability of patterns across recursive transformations, from transient sensory flux to permanent structural or symbolic lattices.
Related Cognitive/Psychological Concept: Pattern recognition; Identity persistence; Cognitive flexibility vs. Stability; Mindset formation.
Classic FEP Capability: Minimization of prediction error/variational free energy to reach internal equilibrium or homeostatic setpoints.
FEP+ Extension Detail: Formalizes torsion curvature and recursive drift as generative fuel; stratifies intelligence into stable particles (Mindons) and uses spectral decomposition to isolate stable structures from turbulence.

Description: The axis of temporal depth and predictive horizons, bridging immediate sensory processing with long-term historical and future-state inference.
Spectrum: Present-Moment (Immediate/Reflexive) to Spatiotemporally Extended (Historical/Future/Arc of History)
Related Cognitive/Psychological Concept: Temporal depth; Prospection; Memory-as-reflection; Long-term planning.
Classic FEP Capability: Active inference used for short-term policy selection and immediate state estimation.
FEP+ Extension Detail: Adds temporal depth through recursive memory fields ("mnēmaic sheaves") and retrocausal modeling; subsumes Free Energy ( $F$ ) and Expected Free Energy ( $EF$ ) into a single construct ( $F^*$ ).
